description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, natural appearance. Its surface displays a striking mix of deep reddish-brown, maroon, and creamy white hues, with visible veins and marbling throughout. The rock has a rough, uneven texture with some angular edges and a few smoother, weathered patches. One side features a prominent white section that appears to be quartz or a similar mineral, contrasting sharply with the darker, iron-rich areas. The overall form is asymmetrical, with no flat surfaces, making it appear as though it was naturally broken or eroded. This type of rock could serve as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, multicolored rock with deep reddish-brown and creamy white marbling, featuring a rough, irregular surface and natural mineral veins.
description_view_0: This view highlights the marbled transition between the reddish-brown and creamy white areas, with a prominent angular edge and visible mineral veins.
description_view_1: The rock appears darker and more uniform in this view, with deep maroon tones and subtle surface indentations, showing less of the white mineral.
description_view_2: This side displays a broad, sloping surface with a balanced mix of reddish-brown and white, and a smoother, more weathered appearance.
description_view_3: The creamy white mineral dominates this view, with a small dark cavity and streaks of red and brown, emphasizing the rock's irregular shape.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
